name: Baked Lasagne
recipeIngredient: 1 large red or green bell pepper, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove sausage casings and crumble the sausage. Brown meat in a large pot, stirring occasionally to break up the meat.
2. Drain off excess fat. Remove the meat and set aside.
3. Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in the same pot. Add onion, garlic, and bell pepper and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally for about 5 to 8 minutes or until the onion is tender.
4. Stir in the reserved meat, tomato puree, tomato paste, water, basil, oregano, sugar (if using), 3/4 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer, stirring occasionally for about 2 hours or until flavors mingle and the mixture thickens slightly (there will be about 4 1/2 to 5 cups of sauce).
5. Combine ricotta, 1/4 cup Parmesan, egg, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper in a medium bowl; set aside.
6. Bring 3 quarts of water to boil in a large pot. Add 1 tablespoon salt, then gradually add lasagne noodles so that the water continues to boil. Cook noodles until barely tender, about 5 minutes, stirring once or twice; do not overcook. Drain noodles; rinse in cold water and drain well. Return noodles to the pot and toss them gently with 1 tablespoon olive oil to prevent sticking.
7. Line the bottom of a buttered 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan with 4 lasagne noodles, cutting one as necessary to completely cover the bottom. Spread evenly with half of the ricotta mixture (1 generous cup).
8. Sprinkle evenly with half of the mozzarella cheese (about 2 cups). Spread evenly with half of the tomato/meat sauce.
9. Top with remaining 4 noodles, cutting one noodle as above so that the sauce is completely covered. Evenly layer on the remaining ingredients in the same order as above.
10. Sprinkle the top of the lasagne evenly with 1/4 cup Parmesan and 1/4 cup mozzarella cheese.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 45 to 50 minutes or until the lasagne is hot in the center. Remove from oven; let stand for 15 minutes before cutting into squares and serving on heated plates with garlic bread.
